{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Developer",
  "description": "Alan &amp; Grant - Our client, a leading pan-African Out-of-Home (OOH) and digital&nbsp;&nbsp;advertising media agency, is seeking to recruit suitably qualified candidates for the position below:&nbsp;

Job Position:&nbsp;Business Developer

Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos&nbsp;

Job Summary


	The Independent Business Developer will be responsible for leading&nbsp;&nbsp;business growth, identifying commercial opportunities, and converting high-value prospects into profitable accounts.
	This is a high-autonomy, execution-focused role suited for a strategic salesperson who can engage directly with corporate decision-makers, manage complex client accounts, and lead competitive tender processes from initial sourcing to final deal closure.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and pursue new business opportunities across corporate, government, and private sector markets.
	Drive direct sales of Alpha &amp; Jam&rsquo;s advertising solutions, including static billboards &amp; digital screens.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, agencies, and strategic partners.
	Source, analyze, and manage tender opportunities from identification to submission and follow-up.
	Develop winning proposals, and partnership offers.
	Prepare high-quality presentations, pitch decks, and client-facing materials to support business acquisition.
	Lead negotiations and close deals in line with company revenue targets.
	Conduct market intelligence and competitor analysis to identify growth opportunities.
	Work closely with internal teams to ensure seamless project execution after deal closure.
	Maintain a healthy pipeline of prospects and provide regular business development reports.


Requirements


	Minimum of 5 years of proven experience in Business Development, Sales, Partnerships.
	Demonstrated ability to source and close high-value deals independently.
	Excellent proposal writing, presentation, and pitching skills.
	Proficiency in PowerPoint, Google Slides, Canva, or similar presentation tools.
	Strong negotiation and stakeholder management skills.
	Good understanding of B2B sales cycles and enterprise account management.
	Experience in advertising, media, digital marketing, or OOH industry is an added advantage.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-07-11",
  "validThrough": "2026-08-12",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Alan & Grant"
    
  },

Business Developer Job at Alan & Grant




        
    

        
        Alan & Grant - Our client, a leading pan-African Out-of-Home (OOH) and digital  advertising media agency, is seeking to recruit suitably qualified candidates for the position below: 

Job Position: Business Developer

Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos 

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Independent Business Developer will be responsible for leading  business growth, identifying commercial opportunities, and converting high-value prospects into profitable accounts.
	This is a high-autonomy, execution-focused role suited for a strategic salesperson who can engage directly with corporate decision-makers, manage complex client accounts, and lead competitive tender processes from initial sourcing to final deal closure.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and pursue new business opportunities across corporate, government, and private sector markets.
	Drive direct sales of Alpha & Jam’s advertising solutions, including static billboards & digital screens.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, agencies, and strategic partners.
	Source, analyze, and manage tender opportunities from identification to submission and follow-up.
	Develop winning proposals, and partnership offers.
	Prepare high-quality presentations, pitch decks, and client-facing materials to support business acquisition.
	Lead negotiations and close deals in line with company revenue targets.
	Conduct market intelligence and competitor analysis to identify growth opportunities.
	Work closely with internal teams to ensure seamless project execution after deal closure.
	Maintain a healthy pipeline of prospects and provide regular business development reports.


Requirements


	Minimum of 5 years of proven experience in Business Development, Sales, Partnerships.
	Demonstrated ability to source and close high-value deals independently.
	Excellent proposal writing, presentation, and pitching skills.
	Proficiency in PowerPoint, Google Slides, Canva, or similar presentation tools.
	Strong negotiation and stakeholder management skills.
	Good understanding of B2B sales cycles and enterprise account management.
	Experience in advertising, media, digital marketing, or OOH industry is an added advantage.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Personal Assistant",
  "description": "Alan &amp; Grant - Our client, a Leading Furniture&nbsp;&nbsp;Manufacturing Firm is seeking to&nbsp;&nbsp;recruit suitably qualified candidates for the position below:&nbsp;

&nbsp;Job Position: Personal Assistant

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	The Personal Assistant to the Managing Director provides dedicated, high-level administrative and strategic support to the MD, ensuring efficient management of schedules, communications, and priorities.
	The role requires exceptional discretion, organization, and coordination to enable the MD to focus on leadership and business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the MD&rsquo;s calendar, appointments, meetings, and travel arrangements.
	Serve as the primary liaison between the MD and internal/external stakeholders.
	Prepare, review, and manage correspondence, reports, presentations, and briefing notes for the MD.
	Organize executive meetings, take minutes, track action points, and follow up to ensure closure.
	Handle confidential and sensitive information with absolute discretion.
	Screen calls, emails, and requests, prioritizing matters requiring the MD&rsquo;s attention.
	Coordinate logistics for board meetings, executive sessions, and official engagements.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Business Administration or a related discipline.
	2 &ndash; 4 years of experience as a Personal Assistant or Executive Assistant supporting senior leadership.
	Strong organizational, time-management, and prioritization skills.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and office productivity tools.
	High level of professionalism, confidentiality, and attention to detail.
	Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities under pressure.

Personal Assistant Job at Alan & Grant




        
    

        
        Alan & Grant - Our client, a Leading Furniture  Manufacturing Firm is seeking to  recruit suitably qualified candidates for the position below: 

 Job Position: Personal Assistant

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Personal Assistant to the Managing Director provides dedicated, high-level administrative and strategic support to the MD, ensuring efficient management of schedules, communications, and priorities.
	The role requires exceptional discretion, organization, and coordination to enable the MD to focus on leadership and business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the MD’s calendar, appointments, meetings, and travel arrangements.
	Serve as the primary liaison between the MD and internal/external stakeholders.
	Prepare, review, and manage correspondence, reports, presentations, and briefing notes for the MD.
	Organize executive meetings, take minutes, track action points, and follow up to ensure closure.
	Handle confidential and sensitive information with absolute discretion.
	Screen calls, emails, and requests, prioritizing matters requiring the MD’s attention.
	Coordinate logistics for board meetings, executive sessions, and official engagements.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration or a related discipline.
	2 – 4 years of experience as a Personal Assistant or Executive Assistant supporting senior leadership.
	Strong organizational, time-management, and prioritization skills.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and office productivity tools.
	High level of professionalism, confidentiality, and attention to detail.
	Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities under pressure.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Officer",
  "description": "Alan &amp; Grant - Our client is a leading Microfinance Bank in Nigeria providing micro-banking services to individuals and SMEs.

They are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:&nbsp;

&nbsp;Job Position: Operations Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Operations Officer is responsible for ensuring the efficient day-to-day operations of the bank by supporting branch and back-office activities, ensuring compliance with operational policies and regulatory requirements, maintaining service quality, and facilitating seamless transaction processing.


Key Responsibilities


	Execute daily operational activities in line with the bank&#39;s policies and procedures.
	Process customer transactions accurately and within established turnaround times.
	Monitor daily branch operations to ensure efficiency and compliance.
	Ensure proper documentation and processing of&nbsp;&nbsp;account opening, account maintenance, and account closure requests.
	Verify customer documentation in line with Know Your Customer (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) requirements.
	Reconcile daily transactions, suspense accounts, and general ledger entries.
	Prepare and submit daily, weekly, and monthly operational reports.
	Monitor cash management activities and ensure adequate cash availability.
	Coordinate the processing of inward and outward transfers, standing orders, and other banking transactions.
	Ensure proper custody and control of sensitive operational instruments, including cheque books, security documents, and vault operations.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Banking &amp; Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, Economics, or a related discipline.
	2 - 5 yearsexperience in banking operations, preferably within a microfinance bank.
	Knowledge of microfinance banking operations and regulatory requirements.
	Professional certifications such as ACIB, ACA, ACCA, or relevant certifications will be an added advantage.
	Strong understanding of banking products, operational controls, and customer service standards.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and Core Banking Systems.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Operations Officer Job at Alan & Grant




        
    

        
        Alan & Grant - Our client is a leading Microfinance Bank in Nigeria providing micro-banking services to individuals and SMEs.

They are  recruiting to fill the position below: 

 Job Position: Operations Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Operations Officer is responsible for ensuring the efficient day-to-day operations of the bank by supporting branch and back-office activities, ensuring compliance with operational policies and regulatory requirements, maintaining service quality, and facilitating seamless transaction processing.


Key Responsibilities


	Execute daily operational activities in line with the bank's policies and procedures.
	Process customer transactions accurately and within established turnaround times.
	Monitor daily branch operations to ensure efficiency and compliance.
	Ensure proper documentation and processing of  account opening, account maintenance, and account closure requests.
	Verify customer documentation in line with Know Your Customer (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) requirements.
	Reconcile daily transactions, suspense accounts, and general ledger entries.
	Prepare and submit daily, weekly, and monthly operational reports.
	Monitor cash management activities and ensure adequate cash availability.
	Coordinate the processing of inward and outward transfers, standing orders, and other banking transactions.
	Ensure proper custody and control of sensitive operational instruments, including cheque books, security documents, and vault operations.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Banking & Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, Economics, or a related discipline.
	2 - 5 yearsexperience in banking operations, preferably within a microfinance bank.
	Knowledge of microfinance banking operations and regulatory requirements.
	Professional certifications such as ACIB, ACA, ACCA, or relevant certifications will be an added advantage.
	Strong understanding of banking products, operational controls, and customer service standards.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and Core Banking Systems.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "UnoCasa is a full service Human Resources&nbsp;&nbsp;Consultancy business with focus on Talent Acquisition, Learning &amp; Development and Consultancy services. Our raison d&rsquo;etre is the notion that your people are your advantage and proffer people-centric solutions to ensure your team accomplish your business goals.&nbsp;

We are not your run of the mill HR Company, we believe in team diversity and teamwork; we believe that the workplace should have an ambience that inspires productivity of the team; and the application of technologies to enhance efficiency. We know that every business is unique and tailor HR solutions to your organisation&rsquo;s needs. Our clients are in various sectors not limited to engineering, education, health oil &amp; gas, government, media, finance, retail, lifestyle,&nbsp;&nbsp;consultancy firms and services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Admin Manager

Job Location: Warri, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Head, Organizational Development

Job Summary


	Are you a strategic and results-driven administrative professional with a strong background in facilities, fleet, and vendor management?
	We are seeking an experienced Admin Manager to lead and optimize our administrative operations, ensuring efficient support for business and project activities within a dynamic, project-driven environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee the day-to-day administration, maintenance, security, and functionality of office facilities.
	Ensure compliance with Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) standards across all administrative operations.
	Manage office space planning, renovations, infrastructure improvements, and office resources.
	Lead fleet operations, including vehicle acquisition, maintenance, fueling, tracking, and regulatory compliance.
	Provide effective logistics support for business operations and project sites.
	Manage vendor selection, contract negotiation, service-level agreements (SLAs), and vendor performance.
	Drive cost-saving initiatives through efficient facilities, fleet, and vendor management practices.
	Implement and maintain document management, archiving, digitization, and records retention systems.
	Ensure proper management of corporate and regulatory compliance documentation.
	Oversee office security systems, access controls, and relationships with security service providers.
	Liaise with government and regulatory agencies to ensure compliance with administrative and facility regulations.
	Lead and develop the administration team through training, succession planning, and continuous improvement initiatives.
	Monitor administrative budgets and ensure operational efficiency while maintaining service quality.


Qualification &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Business Administration, Management, Facilities Management, or a related field.
	8&ndash;10 years of progressive experience in administration, facilities management, fleet management, and vendor management.
	Proven experience within EPC, Oil &amp; Gas, Construction, or other project-based environments.
	Strong track record of managing facilities, logistics, fleets, and vendor contracts.
	Demonstrated success in negotiating service-level agreements (SLAs) and delivering measurable cost savings.
	Knowledge of HSE standards, regulatory compliance, and administrative best practices.
	Proficiency in digital tools for document control, fleet tracking, and vendor management.


Salary

Admin Manager Job at UnoCasa Limited




        
    

        
        UnoCasa is a full service Human Resources  Consultancy business with focus on Talent Acquisition, Learning & Development and Consultancy services. Our raison d’etre is the notion that your people are your advantage and proffer people-centric solutions to ensure your team accomplish your business goals. 
     

            
    



We are not your run of the mill HR Company, we believe in team diversity and teamwork; we believe that the workplace should have an ambience that inspires productivity of the team; and the application of technologies to enhance efficiency. We know that every business is unique and tailor HR solutions to your organisation’s needs. Our clients are in various sectors not limited to engineering, education, health oil & gas, government, media, finance, retail, lifestyle,  consultancy firms and services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Admin Manager

Job Location: Warri, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Head, Organizational Development

Job Summary


	Are you a strategic and results-driven administrative professional with a strong background in facilities, fleet, and vendor management?
	We are seeking an experienced Admin Manager to lead and optimize our administrative operations, ensuring efficient support for business and project activities within a dynamic, project-driven environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee the day-to-day administration, maintenance, security, and functionality of office facilities.
	Ensure compliance with Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) standards across all administrative operations.
	Manage office space planning, renovations, infrastructure improvements, and office resources.
	Lead fleet operations, including vehicle acquisition, maintenance, fueling, tracking, and regulatory compliance.
	Provide effective logistics support for business operations and project sites.
	Manage vendor selection, contract negotiation, service-level agreements (SLAs), and vendor performance.
	Drive cost-saving initiatives through efficient facilities, fleet, and vendor management practices.
	Implement and maintain document management, archiving, digitization, and records retention systems.
	Ensure proper management of corporate and regulatory compliance documentation.
	Oversee office security systems, access controls, and relationships with security service providers.
	Liaise with government and regulatory agencies to ensure compliance with administrative and facility regulations.
	Lead and develop the administration team through training, succession planning, and continuous improvement initiatives.
	Monitor administrative budgets and ensure operational efficiency while maintaining service quality.


Qualification & Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Management, Facilities Management, or a related field.
	8–10 years of progressive experience in administration, facilities management, fleet management, and vendor management.
	Proven experience within EPC, Oil & Gas, Construction, or other project-based environments.
	Strong track record of managing facilities, logistics, fleets, and vendor contracts.
	Demonstrated success in negotiating service-level agreements (SLAs) and delivering measurable cost savings.
	Knowledge of HSE standards, regulatory compliance, and administrative best practices.
	Proficiency in digital tools for document control, fleet tracking, and vendor management.


Salary
N1,800,000 - N2,300,000 gross monthly.    

    

        Click here to Apply
